nodes.label: Ticket
nodes.rationale: One unit of work the board tracks; carries Kind, Column, Stage and agent knobs. All mutations go through store.ts.

nodes.label: Kind (ticket pipeline type)
nodes.rationale: What pipeline a ticket runs: feature, review, clean, ask. Conflict resolution is a mode on a feature ticket, not a kind.

nodes.label: Column (board lane)
nodes.rationale: The coarse, user-visible board lane axis (8: todo, implementing, prd, done, merged, reviewed, failed, abandoned).

nodes.label: Stage (pipeline state)
nodes.rationale: Fine-grained pipeline state, a different axis from Column (12 stages). Use ACTIVE_STAGES/TERMINAL_STAGES, never hardcode. Subset is agent-settable; terminal stages are backend-set only.

nodes.label: Slot (git-worktree execution unit)
nodes.rationale: A git-worktree execution unit from a fixed pool (SLOT_COUNT) at SLOTS_ROOT/slot-N. Git ops per repo serialize through repoMutex.

nodes.label: SlotManager
nodes.rationale: Owns slot lifecycle: cleanup -> fetch -> worktree add -> install -> start SDK session -> inject contract -> done gate -> release. No free slot -> FIFO queue.

nodes.label: SessionHub
nodes.rationale: Owns each live SDK agent session (query() streaming-input, in-process). Injects channel events as user turns, exposes worker tools as an in-process MCP server, keeps the per-session live transcript. Replaced the old worker.ts + WorkerHub WS bridge.

nodes.label: Channel (agent<->backend link)
nodes.rationale: Two-way agent<->backend link. backend->agent: channel events (ticket, answer, prd_validated, nudge, user_comment). agent->backend: worker tool calls.

nodes.label: Protocol (wire format source of truth)
nodes.rationale: Single source of truth for the Channel wire format (tool registry, channel-event union, WS frames) in src/shared/protocol.ts so worker, coordinator, and web client derive from one declaration instead of hand-synced copies. Introduced by architecture candidate #2 (the wire-protocol seam).

nodes.label: Contract (pipeline instructions)
nodes.rationale: Pipeline instructions (contract.ts) injected verbatim as the first ticket channel event. The backend never reasons; the contract tells the agent's own native subagents what to do.

nodes.label: Done Gate
nodes.rationale: Server-side verification of done(pr_url): clean tree, branch pushed, PR exists. The backend does not trust the agent. Failure -> stalled, slot kept.

nodes.label: Coordinator
nodes.rationale: Routes worker tool calls and Stop-hook/turn-end events to state mutations; owns the auto-nudge -> auto-reclaim -> stalled escalation.

nodes.label: Ticket Lifecycle
nodes.rationale: Deep module above store (src/server/lifecycle.ts) whose verbs fuse the coupling each transition carries (ticket fields + slot status + finishedAt) with the broadcast/log/notify ritual so a caller can't move stage without syncing slot or forget to push. Architecture candidate #1. Does NOT depend on SessionHub.

nodes.label: System Adapter (side-effect seam)
nodes.rationale: The single side-effect seam (git, tmux, gh, osascript, fs, ~/.claude.json) at src/server/system/. One injectable interface (SystemAdapter) with two implementations.

nodes.label: Triage / Feasibility
nodes.rationale: Read-only implementability analysis run before implementation. Triage runs one session on the real repo; feasibility fans out one batch session over many imported tickets.
